WHERE e.is_active = TRUE AND e.role IN ('Teller', 'Loan Officer', 'Manager', 'Investment Analyst', 'Risk Analyst')

query: For each customer with an active loan and active portfolio, show name, total outstanding debt, portfolio value, and net position.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT l.outstanding_balance),2) AS total_debt, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT p.total_value),2) AS total_portfolio, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT p.total_value)-SUM(DISTINCT l.outstanding_balance),2) AS net_position FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id JOIN portfolios p ON p.customer_id=c.id WHERE l.status='active' AND p.is_active=1 GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name ORDER BY net_position DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id JOIN portfolios p ON c.id = p.customer_id WHERE l.status = 'active' AND p.is_active = TRUE;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, c.email FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id JOIN portfolios p ON c.id = p.customer_id WHERE l.status = 'active' AND p.is_active = TRUE;

query: Which branches have a loan default rate above 20%? Show branch name, total loans, defaulted count, and default rate %.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT b.name, COUNT(l.id) AS total_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaulted, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(l.id),2) AS default_rate_pct FROM loans l JOIN branches b ON l.branch_id=b.id GROUP BY b.id,b.name HAVING default_rate_pct>20 ORDER BY default_rate_pct DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT branches.name, COUNT(loans.id) AS total_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaulted_count, 
       (SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(loans.id)) AS default_rate_percentage
FROM branches 
JOIN customers ON branches.id = customers.branch_id 
JOIN loans ON branches.id = loans.branch_id 
GROUP BY branches.name 
HAVING (SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(loans.id)) > 20;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT branches.name, COUNT(loans.id) AS total_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaulted_count, 
       (SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(loans.id)) AS default_rate_percentage
FROM branches 
JOIN customers ON branches.id = customers.branch_id 
JOIN loans ON branches.id = loans.branch_id 
GROUP BY branches.name 
HAVING (SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(loans.id)) > 20;

query: Calculate unrealized P&L percentage for each asset class across all active portfolios. Show asset class, total current value, cost basis, and P&L %.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT a.asset_class, ROUND(SUM(ph.current_value),2) AS current_val, ROUND(SUM(ph.quantity*ph.avg_buy_price),2) AS cost_basis, ROUND(100.0*(SUM(ph.current_value)-SUM(ph.quantity*ph.avg_buy_price))/NULLIF(SUM(ph.quantity*ph.avg_buy_price),0),2) AS pnl_pct FROM portfolio_holdings ph JOIN assets a ON ph.asset_id=a.id JOIN portfolios p ON ph.portfolio_id=p.id WHERE p.is_active=1 GROUP BY a.asset_class ORDER BY pnl_pct DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT 
    assets.asset_class, 
    SUM(portfolio_holdings.current_value) AS total_current_value, 
    SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity) AS cost_basis, 
    (SUM(portfolio_holdings.current_value) - SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity)) / NULLIF(SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity), 0) * 100 AS pnl_percentage 
FROM 
    assets 
JOIN 
    market_prices ON assets.id = market_prices.asset_id 
JOIN 
    portfolio_holdings ON assets.id = portfolio_holdings.asset_id 
JOIN 
    portfolios ON portfolio_holdings.portfolio_id = portfolios.id 
WHERE 
    portfolios.is_active = TRUE 
GROUP BY 
    assets.asset_class;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT 
    assets.asset_class, 
    SUM(portfolio_holdings.current_value) AS total_current_value, 
    SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity) AS cost_basis, 
    (SUM(portfolio_holdings.current_value) - SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity)) / NULLIF(SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity), 0) * 100 AS pnl_percentage 
FROM 
    assets 
JOIN 
    market_prices ON assets.id = market_prices.asset_id 
JOIN 
    portfolio_holdings ON assets.id = portfolio_holdings.asset_id 
JOIN 
    portfolios ON portfolio_holdings.portfolio_id = portfolios.id 
WHERE 
    portfolios.is_active = TRUE 
GROUP BY 
    assets.asset_class;

query: List the top 10 customers by total transaction volume (all completed transactions). Include branch, tx count, and total volume.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, b.name AS branch, COUNT(t.id) AS tx_count, ROUND(SUM(t.amount),2) AS total_volume FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id=a.id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id=c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id=b.id WHERE t.status='completed' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name,b.name ORDER BY total_volume DESC LIMIT 10;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT c.full_name, b.name AS branch, COUNT(t.id) AS transaction_count, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume 
FROM transactions t 
JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.id 
JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id 
JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id 
WHERE t.status = 'completed' 
GROUP BY c.id, c.full_name, b.name 
ORDER BY total_volume DESC 
LIMIT 10;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, b.name AS branch, COUNT(t.id) AS transaction_count, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id GROUP BY c.id, c.full_name, b.id, b.name ORDER BY total_volume DESC LIMIT 10;

query: Identify customers with more than 3 late loan payments who are in the Very High risk tier. Show name, late payment count, avg days late, and relationship manager.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, e.full_name AS rm, COUNT(lp.id) AS late_count, ROUND(AVG(lp.days_late),1) AS avg_days_late, r.risk_tier FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id JOIN loan_payments lp ON lp.loan_id=l.id JOIN risk_assessments r ON r.customer_id=c.id JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id=e.id WHERE lp.days_late>0 AND r.risk_tier='Very High' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name,e.full_name,r.risk_tier HAVING COUNT(lp.id)>3 ORDER BY late_count DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, COUNT(lp.id) AS late_payment_count, AVG(lp.days_late) AS average_days_late, e.full_name AS relationship_manager FROM customers c JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id = e.id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id JOIN loan_payments lp ON l.id = lp.loan_id JOIN risk_assessments ra ON c.id = ra.customer_id WHERE lp.status = 'late' AND ra.risk_tier = 'Very High' GROUP BY c.full_name, e.full_name HAVING COUNT(lp.id) > 3;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, COUNT(lp.id) AS late_payment_count, AVG(lp.days_late) AS average_days_late, e.full_name AS relationship_manager FROM customers c JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id = e.id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id JOIN loan_payments lp ON l.id = lp.loan_id JOIN risk_assessments ra ON c.id = ra.customer_id WHERE lp.status = 'late' AND ra.risk_tier = 'Very High' GROUP BY c.full_name, e.full_name HAVING COUNT(lp.id) > 3;

ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, a.currency, ROUND(SUM(a.balance),2) AS total_balance FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id=c.id WHERE a.status='active' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name,a.currency HAVING c.id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM accounts WHERE status='active' GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT currency)>1) ORDER BY c.full_name,a.currency;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, a.currency, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.full_name, a.currency HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.currency) > 1;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, a.currency, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.full_name, a.currency HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.currency) > 1;

query: Produce a credit risk summary per branch: customer count, avg credit score, count per risk tier (Low/Medium/High/Very High), and avg default probability.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT b.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS customers, ROUND(AVG(r.credit_score),1) AS avg_score, SUM(CASE WHEN r.risk_tier='Low' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) low_risk, SUM(CASE WHEN r.risk_tier='Medium' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) medium_risk, SUM(CASE WHEN r.risk_tier='High' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) high_risk, SUM(CASE WHEN r.risk_tier='Very High' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) very_high_risk, ROUND(AVG(r.default_probability),4) AS avg_default_prob FROM risk_assessments r JOIN customers c ON r.customer_id=c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id=b.id GROUP BY b.id,b.name ORDER BY avg_default_prob DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT branches.name, COUNT(DISTINCT customers.id) AS customer_count, AVG(risk_assessments.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN risk_assessments.risk_tier = 'Low' THEN 1 END) AS low_count,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN risk_assessments.risk_tier = 'Medium' THEN 1 END) AS medium_count,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN risk_assessments.risk_tier = 'High' THEN 1 END) AS high_count,
       COUNT(CASE WHEN risk_assessments.risk_tier = 'Very High' THEN 1 END) AS very_high_count,
       AVG(risk_assessments.default_probability) AS avg_default_probability
FROM branches 
JOIN customers ON branches.id = customers.branch_id 
JOIN loans ON customers.id = loans.customer_id 
JOIN risk_assessments ON loans.id = risk_assessments.loan_id 
GROUP BY branches.name;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT b.name AS branch_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS customer_count, AVG(ra.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Low' THEN 1 END) AS low_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Medium' THEN 1 END) AS medium_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'High' THEN 1 END) AS high_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Very High' THEN 1 END) AS very_high_count, 
       AVG(ra.default_probability) AS avg_default_probability 
FROM branches b 
JOIN customers c ON b.id = c.branch_id 
JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id 
JOIN risk_assessments ra ON l.id = ra.loan_id 
GROUP BY b.name 
ORDER BY branch_name;

query: For each customer with both an active loan and active portfolio, show name, total outstanding debt, total portfolio value, and net position (portfolio minus debt).
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT l.outstanding_balance),2) AS total_debt, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT p.total_value),2) AS total_portfolio, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT p.total_value)-SUM(DISTINCT l.outstanding_balance),2) AS net_position FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id JOIN portfolios p ON p.customer_id=c.id WHERE l.status='active' AND p.is_active=1 GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name ORDER BY net_position DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT DISTINCT c.full_name, l.status, p.is_active
FROM customers c
JOIN accounts a ON a.customer_id = c.id
JOIN loans l ON a.id = l.disbursement_account_id
JOIN portfolios p ON a.id = p.funding_account_id;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT DISTINCT c.full_name
FROM customers c
JOIN accounts a ON a.customer_id = c.id
JOIN loans l ON a.id = l.disbursement_account_id
JOIN portfolios p ON a.id = p.funding_account_id
WHERE l.status = 'active' AND p.is_active = TRUE;

query: Identify customers with more than 3 late loan payments who are in the Very High risk tier. Show name, late count, avg days late, and relationship manager.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, e.full_name AS rm, COUNT(lp.id) AS late_count, ROUND(AVG(lp.days_late),1) AS avg_days_late FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id JOIN loan_payments lp ON lp.loan_id=l.id JOIN risk_assessments r ON r.customer_id=c.id JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id=e.id WHERE lp.days_late>0 AND r.risk_tier='Very High' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name,e.full_name HAVING COUNT(lp.id)>3 ORDER BY late_count DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, COUNT(lp.id) AS late_count, AVG(lp.days_late) AS average_days_late, e.full_name AS relationship_manager FROM loan_payments lp JOIN loans l ON lp.loan_id = l.id JOIN customers c ON l.customer_id = c.id JOIN risk_assessments ra ON c.id = ra.customer_id JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id = e.id WHERE lp.status = 'late' AND ra.risk_tier = 'Very High' GROUP BY c.full_name, e.full_name HAVING COUNT(lp.id) > 3;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, COUNT(lp.id) AS late_count, AVG(lp.days_late) AS average_days_late, e.full_name AS relationship_manager FROM loan_payments lp JOIN loans l ON lp.loan_id = l.id JOIN customers c ON l.customer_id = c.id JOIN risk_assessments ra ON c.id = ra.customer_id JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id = e.id WHERE lp.status = 'late' AND ra.risk_tier = 'Very High' GROUP BY c.full_name, e.full_name HAVING COUNT(lp.id) > 3;

query: Calculate unrealized P&L percentage per asset class across all active portfolios. Show asset class, total current value, total cost basis, and P&L %.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT a.asset_class, ROUND(SUM(ph.current_value),2) AS current_val, ROUND(SUM(ph.quantity*ph.avg_buy_price),2) AS cost_basis, ROUND(100.0*(SUM(ph.current_value)-SUM(ph.quantity*ph.avg_buy_price))/NULLIF(SUM(ph.quantity*ph.avg_buy_price),0),2) AS pnl_pct FROM portfolio_holdings ph JOIN assets a ON ph.asset_id=a.id JOIN portfolios p ON ph.portfolio_id=p.id WHERE p.is_active=1 GROUP BY a.asset_class ORDER BY pnl_pct DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT 
    assets.asset_class, 
    SUM(portfolio_holdings.current_value) AS total_current_value, 
    SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity) AS cost_basis, 
    (SUM(portfolio_holdings.current_value) - SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity)) / NULLIF(SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity), 0) * 100 AS pnl_percentage 
FROM 
    assets 
JOIN 
    market_prices ON assets.id = market_prices.asset_id 
JOIN 
    portfolio_holdings ON assets.id = portfolio_holdings.asset_id 
JOIN 
    portfolios ON portfolio_holdings.portfolio_id = portfolios.id 
WHERE 
    portfolios.is_active = TRUE 
GROUP BY 
    assets.asset_class;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT 
    assets.asset_class, 
    SUM(portfolio_holdings.current_value) AS total_current_value, 
    SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity) AS cost_basis, 
    (SUM(portfolio_holdings.current_value) - SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity)) / NULLIF(SUM(portfolio_holdings.avg_buy_price * portfolio_holdings.quantity), 0) * 100 AS pnl_percentage 
FROM 
    assets 
JOIN 
    market_prices ON assets.id = market_prices.asset_id 
JOIN 
    portfolio_holdings ON assets.id = portfolio_holdings.asset_id 
JOIN 
    portfolios ON portfolio_holdings.portfolio_id = portfolios.id 
WHERE 
    portfolios.is_active = TRUE 
GROUP BY 
    assets.asset_class;

query: Which branches have a loan default rate above 20%? Show branch, total loans, defaulted count, and default rate %.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT b.name, COUNT(l.id) AS total_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaulted, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN l.status='defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(l.id),2) AS default_rate_pct FROM loans l JOIN branches b ON l.branch_id=b.id GROUP BY b.id,b.name HAVING default_rate_pct>20 ORDER BY default_rate_pct DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT branches.name, COUNT(loans.id) AS total_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaulted_count, 
       (SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(loans.id)) AS default_rate
FROM branches 
JOIN customers ON branches.id = customers.branch_id 
JOIN loans ON branches.id = loans.branch_id 
GROUP BY branches.name 
HAVING (SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(loans.id)) > 20;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT branches.name, COUNT(loans.id) AS total_loans, SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS defaulted_count, 
       (SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(loans.id)) AS default_rate
FROM branches 
JOIN customers ON branches.id = customers.branch_id 
JOIN loans ON branches.id = loans.branch_id 
GROUP BY branches.name 
HAVING (SUM(CASE WHEN loans.status = 'defaulted' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(loans.id)) > 20;

query: Find customers holding accounts in more than one currency. Show name, currencies, and total balance per currency.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, a.currency, ROUND(SUM(a.balance),2) AS total_balance FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id=c.id WHERE a.status='active' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name,a.currency HAVING c.id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM accounts WHERE status='active' GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT currency)>1) ORDER BY c.full_name,a.currency;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, a.currency, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.full_name, a.currency HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.currency) > 1;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, a.currency, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.full_name, a.currency HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.currency) > 1;

query: Produce a full credit risk summary per branch: customer count, avg credit score, count per tier (Low/Medium/High/Very High), and avg default probability.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT b.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS customers, ROUND(AVG(r.credit_score),1) AS avg_score, SUM(CASE WHEN r.risk_tier='Low' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) low_risk, SUM(CASE WHEN r.risk_tier='Medium' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) medium_risk, SUM(CASE WHEN r.risk_tier='High' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) high_risk, SUM(CASE WHEN r.risk_tier='Very High' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) very_high_risk, ROUND(AVG(r.default_probability),4) AS avg_default_prob FROM risk_assessments r JOIN customers c ON r.customer_id=c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id=b.id GROUP BY b.id,b.name ORDER BY avg_default_prob DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT b.name AS branch_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS customer_count, AVG(ra.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Low' THEN 1 END) AS low_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Medium' THEN 1 END) AS medium_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'High' THEN 1 END) AS high_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Very High' THEN 1 END) AS very_high_count, 
       AVG(ra.default_probability) AS avg_default_probability 
FROM branches b 
JOIN customers c ON b.id = c.branch_id 
JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id 
JOIN risk_assessments ra ON l.id = ra.loan_id 
GROUP BY b.name 
ORDER BY branch_name;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT b.name AS branch_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS customer_count, AVG(ra.credit_score) AS avg_credit_score, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Low' THEN 1 END) AS low_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Medium' THEN 1 END) AS medium_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'High' THEN 1 END) AS high_count, 
       COUNT(CASE WHEN ra.risk_tier = 'Very High' THEN 1 END) AS very_high_count, 
       AVG(ra.default_probability) AS avg_default_probability 
FROM branches b 
JOIN customers c ON b.id = c.branch_id 
JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id 
JOIN risk_assessments ra ON l.id = ra.loan_id 
GROUP BY b.name 
ORDER BY b.name;

query: For each Loan Officer, show how many active loans they manage, total principal, and average interest rate.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.full_name, b.name AS branch, COUNT(l.id) AS active_loans, ROUND(SUM(l.principal),2) AS total_principal, ROUND(AVG(l.interest_rate),2) AS avg_rate FROM loans l JOIN customers c ON l.customer_id=c.id JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id=e.id JOIN branches b ON e.branch_id=b.id WHERE e.role='Loan Officer' AND l.status='active' GROUP BY e.id,e.full_name,b.name ORDER BY total_principal DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT e.full_name, COUNT(l.id) AS active_loans FROM employees e JOIN customers c ON e.id = c.relationship_manager_id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id WHERE e.is_active = TRUE AND l.status != 'defaulted' GROUP BY e.full_name;
full_sql_executed: SELECT e.full_name, AVG(l.interest_rate) AS average_interest_rate FROM employees e JOIN customers c ON e.id = c.relationship_manager_id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id GROUP BY e.full_name;

query: Show assets whose average market price change was negative over all recorded periods, ranked worst to best.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT a.symbol, a.name, a.asset_class, ROUND(AVG(mp.change_pct),4) AS avg_change FROM market_prices mp JOIN assets a ON mp.asset_id=a.id GROUP BY a.id,a.symbol,a.name,a.asset_class HAVING AVG(mp.change_pct)<0 ORDER BY avg_change ASC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT assets.symbol, assets.name, AVG(market_prices.change_pct) AS avg_price_change
FROM assets 
JOIN market_prices ON assets.id = market_prices.asset_id 
GROUP BY assets.id
HAVING AVG(market_prices.change_pct) < 0;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT assets.symbol, assets.name, AVG(market_prices.change_pct) AS avg_price_change
FROM assets 
JOIN market_prices ON assets.id = market_prices.asset_id 
JOIN portfolio_holdings ON assets.id = portfolio_holdings.asset_id
GROUP BY assets.id
HAVING AVG(market_prices.change_pct) < 0
ORDER BY avg_price_change ASC;

query: List the top 10 customers by total completed transaction volume, including branch and tx count.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, b.name AS branch, COUNT(t.id) AS tx_count, ROUND(SUM(t.amount),2) AS total_volume FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id=a.id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id=c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id=b.id WHERE t.status='completed' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name,b.name ORDER BY total_volume DESC LIMIT 10;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, b.name AS branch, COUNT(t.id) AS transaction_count, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id WHERE t.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.full_name, b.name ORDER BY total_volume DESC LIMIT 10;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, b.name AS branch, COUNT(t.id) AS transaction_count, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id WHERE t.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.full_name, b.name ORDER BY total_volume DESC LIMIT 10;

query: Identify customers who qualify for Premium wealth management (combined EGP account balance + portfolio value ≥ EGP 5,000,000). Show relationship value.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT a.balance),2) AS acct_balance, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT p.total_value),2) AS portfolio_value, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT a.balance)+SUM(DISTINCT p.total_value),2) AS total_rv FROM customers c LEFT JOIN accounts a ON a.customer_id=c.id AND a.status='active' AND a.currency='EGP' LEFT JOIN portfolios p ON p.customer_id=c.id AND p.is_active=1 GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name HAVING total_rv>=5000000 ORDER BY total_rv DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT DISTINCT c.full_name, c.email, c.phone, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance, SUM(p.total_value) AS total_portfolio_value
FROM customers c
JOIN accounts a ON c.id = a.customer_id
JOIN portfolios p ON a.id = p.funding_account_id
WHERE a.currency = 'EGP' AND a.status = 'active' AND p.is_active = TRUE
GROUP BY c.id
HAVING SUM(a.balance) + SUM(p.total_value) >= 1000000;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT DISTINCT c.full_name, c.email, c.phone, SUM(a.balance) AS total_balance, SUM(p.total_value) AS total_portfolio_value
FROM customers c
JOIN accounts a ON c.id = a.customer_id
JOIN portfolios p ON a.id = p.funding_account_id
WHERE a.currency = 'EGP' AND a.status = 'active' AND p.is_active = TRUE
GROUP BY c.id
HAVING SUM(a.balance) + SUM(p.total_value) >= 1000000;

query: For each transaction category, show total transaction count, total volume, and percentage share of overall transaction volume.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT tc.name, COUNT(t.id) AS cnt, ROUND(SUM(t.amount),2) AS total_vol, ROUND(100.0*SUM(t.amount)/(SELECT SUM(amount) FROM transactions WHERE status='completed'),2) AS pct_share FROM transactions t JOIN transaction_categories tc ON t.category_id=tc.id WHERE t.status='completed' GROUP BY tc.name ORDER BY total_vol DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT tc.name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume, (SUM(t.amount) / (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM transactions) * 100) AS percentage_share FROM transaction_categories tc JOIN transactions t ON tc.id = t.category_id GROUP BY tc.id, tc.name ORDER BY total_volume DESC;
full_sql_executed: SELECT tc.name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume, (SUM(t.amount) / (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM transactions) * 100) AS percentage_share FROM transaction_categories tc JOIN transactions t ON tc.id = t.category_id GROUP BY tc.id, tc.name ORDER BY total_volume DESC;

query: Show the monthly principal vs interest breakdown of all loan repayments in 2024 across the entire portfolio.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT STRFTIME('%Y-%m',payment_date) AS month, ROUND(SUM(principal_portion),2) AS total_principal, ROUND(SUM(interest_portion),2) AS total_interest, ROUND(SUM(amount_paid),2) AS total_collected FROM loan_payments WHERE STRFTIME('%Y',payment_date)='2024' AND status!='missed' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, lp.payment_date, lp.principal_portion, lp.interest_portion FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id JOIN loan_payments lp ON l.id = lp.loan_id WHERE lp.payment_date >= '2024-01-01' AND lp.payment_date < '2025-01-01' ORDER BY lp.payment_date;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, lp.payment_date, lp.principal_portion, lp.interest_portion FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id JOIN loan_payments lp ON l.id = lp.loan_id WHERE lp.payment_date >= '2024-01-01' AND lp.payment_date < '2025-01-01' ORDER BY lp.payment_date;

query: For each branch, show the number of customers with no active loans, no active accounts, and no active portfolios simultaneously (fully dormant customers).
ground_truth_sql: SELECT b.name, COUNT(c.id) AS dormant_customers FROM customers c JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id=b.id WHERE c.is_active=1 AND c.id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM loans WHERE status='active') AND c.id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM accounts WHERE status='active') AND c.id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM portfolios WHERE is_active=1) GROUP BY b.id,b.name ORDER BY dormant_customers DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT b.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS customer_count FROM customers c JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id LEFT JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id AND l.status = 'active' LEFT JOIN accounts a ON c.id = a.customer_id AND a.status = 'active' LEFT JOIN portfolios p ON c.id = p.customer_id AND p.is_active = TRUE WHERE l.id IS NULL AND a.id IS NULL AND p.id IS NULL GROUP BY b.name;
full_sql_executed: SELECT b.name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.id) AS customer_count FROM branches b JOIN customers c ON b.id = c.branch_id LEFT JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id AND l.status = 'active' LEFT JOIN accounts a ON c.id = a.customer_id AND a.status = 'active' LEFT JOIN portfolios p ON c.id = p.customer_id AND p.is_active = TRUE WHERE l.id IS NULL AND a.id IS NULL AND p.id IS NULL GROUP BY b.name;

query: Compute the weighted average interest rate of the active loan book, weighted by outstanding balance, broken down by loan type.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT lt.name, ROUND(SUM(l.interest_rate*l.outstanding_balance)/NULLIF(SUM(l.outstanding_balance),0),4) AS weighted_avg_rate, ROUND(SUM(l.outstanding_balance),2) AS total_outstanding FROM loans l JOIN loan_types lt ON l.loan_type_id=lt.id WHERE l.status='active' GROUP BY lt.name ORDER BY total_outstanding DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT lt.name AS loan_type, SUM(l.outstanding_balance * l.interest_rate) / NULLIF(SUM(l.outstanding_balance), 0) AS weighted_average_interest_rate FROM loan_types lt JOIN loans l ON lt.id = l.loan_type_id WHERE l.status = 'active' GROUP BY lt.id, lt.name ORDER BY weighted_average_interest_rate DESC;
full_sql_executed: SELECT lt.name AS loan_type, SUM(l.outstanding_balance) AS total_outstanding, SUM(l.outstanding_balance * l.interest_rate) / NULLIF(SUM(l.outstanding_balance), 0) AS weighted_average_interest_rate FROM loan_types lt JOIN loans l ON lt.id = l.loan_type_id WHERE l.status = 'active' GROUP BY lt.id, lt.name ORDER BY weighted_average_interest_rate DESC;

query: List all customers who have made a Salary Credit transaction but do not have an active savings or premium savings account — potential cross-sell opportunity.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.full_name, c.city FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON a.customer_id=c.id JOIN transactions t ON t.account_id=a.id JOIN transaction_categories tc ON t.category_id=tc.id WHERE tc.name='Salary Credit' AND c.id NOT IN (SELECT ac.customer_id FROM accounts ac JOIN account_types aty ON ac.account_type_id=aty.id WHERE aty.name IN ('Savings Account','Premium Savings') AND ac.status='active');
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, c.email FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.id = a.customer_id JOIN account_types at ON a.account_type_id = at.id WHERE a.status != 'active' OR at.name != 'Premium Savings' GROUP BY c.id;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name FROM customers c JOIN accounts a ON c.id = a.customer_id WHERE a.account_type_id IS NULL OR a.status != 'active' AND a.account_type_id IN (SELECT id FROM account_types WHERE name = 'savings') GROUP BY c.full_name;

query: For each Investment Analyst, show the total portfolio value they manage and the average portfolio performance (total unrealized P&L / total cost basis).
ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.full_name AS analyst, ROUND(SUM(p.total_value),2) AS total_aum, ROUND(100.0*SUM(ph.unrealized_pnl)/NULLIF(SUM(ph.quantity*ph.avg_buy_price),0),2) AS avg_pnl_pct FROM employees e JOIN customers c ON c.relationship_manager_id=e.id JOIN portfolios p ON p.customer_id=c.id JOIN portfolio_holdings ph ON ph.portfolio_id=p.id WHERE e.role='Investment Analyst' AND p.is_active=1 GROUP BY e.id,e.full_name ORDER BY total_aum DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT e.full_name AS "Investment Analyst", SUM(p.total_value) AS "Total Portfolio Value"
FROM customers c
JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id = e.id
JOIN portfolios p ON c.id = p.customer_id
WHERE e.role = 'Investment Analyst'
GROUP BY e.full_name;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT e.full_name AS "Investment Analyst", SUM(p.total_value) AS "Total Portfolio Value"
FROM customers c
JOIN employees e ON c.relationship_manager_id = e.id
JOIN portfolios p ON c.id = p.customer_id
WHERE e.role = 'Investment Analyst'
GROUP BY e.full_name;

query: Show all customers whose total debit transactions in the last recorded month exceed EGP 200,000, ranked by total debit amount.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, ROUND(SUM(t.amount),2) AS total_debit FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id=a.id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id=c.id WHERE t.direction='debit' AND t.status='completed' AND STRFTIME('%Y-%m',t.transaction_date)=(SELECT MAX(STRFTIME('%Y-%m',transaction_date)) FROM transactions) GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name HAVING SUM(t.amount)>200000 ORDER BY total_debit DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT customers.full_name, SUM(transactions.amount) AS total_debit
FROM accounts
JOIN customers ON accounts.customer_id = customers.id
JOIN transactions ON accounts.id = transactions.account_id
WHERE transactions.direction = 'debit' 
AND transactions.transaction_date = (SELECT MAX(transaction_date) FROM transactions)
GROUP BY customers.id
HAVING SUM(transactions.amount) > 200000
ORDER BY total_debit DESC;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, SUM(t.amount) AS total_debits FROM transactions t JOIN accounts a ON t.account_id = a.id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id WHERE t.direction = 'debit' AND t.status = 'completed' AND t.transaction_date >= date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE) - interval '1 month' AND t.transaction_date < date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY c.id, c.full_name HAVING SUM(t.amount) > 200000 ORDER BY total_debits DESC;

query: For each asset, show price on the first and last recorded date, total price change, and total trading volume across all recorded days.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT a.symbol, a.name, MIN(mp.price_date) AS first_date, MAX(mp.price_date) AS last_date, ROUND(MIN(CASE WHEN mp.price_date=(SELECT MIN(price_date) FROM market_prices mp2 WHERE mp2.asset_id=a.id) THEN mp.price END),4) AS first_price, ROUND(MAX(CASE WHEN mp.price_date=(SELECT MAX(price_date) FROM market_prices mp2 WHERE mp2.asset_id=a.id) THEN mp.price END),4) AS last_price, SUM(mp.volume) AS total_volume FROM market_prices mp JOIN assets a ON mp.asset_id=a.id GROUP BY a.id,a.symbol,a.name ORDER BY a.symbol;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT assets.symbol, SUM(market_prices.price) AS total_price_change
FROM assets
JOIN market_prices ON assets.id = market_prices.asset_id
GROUP BY assets.symbol;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT assets.symbol, market_prices.price
FROM assets
JOIN market_prices ON assets.id = market_prices.asset_id
WHERE market_prices.price_date = (SELECT MAX(price_date) FROM market_prices WHERE asset_id = assets.id);

query: Find all customers with more than one loan of different types currently active. Show customer name, loan types, and total outstanding across all loans.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT lt.name) AS loan_types, COUNT(l.id) AS loan_count, ROUND(SUM(l.outstanding_balance),2) AS total_outstanding FROM customers c JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id JOIN loan_types lt ON l.loan_type_id=lt.id WHERE l.status='active' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.loan_type_id)>1 ORDER BY total_outstanding DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, lt.name AS loan_type, SUM(l.outstanding_balance) AS total_outstanding FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id JOIN loans l ON a.id = l.disbursement_account_id JOIN loan_types lt ON l.loan_type_id = lt.id WHERE l.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.full_name, lt.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.loan_type_id) > 1;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, lt.name AS loan_type, SUM(l.outstanding_balance) AS total_outstanding FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id JOIN loans l ON a.id = l.disbursement_account_id JOIN loan_types lt ON l.loan_type_id = lt.id WHERE l.status = 'active' GROUP BY c.full_name, lt.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.loan_type_id) > 1;

query: Show the repayment performance per loan officer: total loans managed, % on-time payments, % late payments, % missed payments.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.full_name AS loan_officer, COUNT(DISTINCT l.id) AS total_loans, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN lp.status='on_time' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(lp.id),0),2) AS pct_on_time, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN lp.status='late' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(lp.id),0),2) AS pct_late, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN lp.status='missed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/NULLIF(COUNT(lp.id),0),2) AS pct_missed FROM employees e JOIN customers c ON c.relationship_manager_id=e.id JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id JOIN loan_payments lp ON lp.loan_id=l.id WHERE e.role='Loan Officer' GROUP BY e.id,e.full_name ORDER BY pct_missed DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT e.full_name, COUNT(lp.id) FILTER (WHERE lp.status = 'missed') * 100.0 / COUNT(lp.id) AS missed_payment_percentage FROM employees e JOIN customers c ON e.id = c.relationship_manager_id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id JOIN loan_payments lp ON l.id = lp.loan_id GROUP BY e.full_name;
full_sql_executed: SELECT e.full_name, COUNT(lp.id) AS missed_payments, COUNT(l.id) AS total_loans, (COUNT(lp.id) * 100.0 / COUNT(l.id)) AS percentage_missed_payments FROM employees e JOIN customers c ON e.id = c.relationship_manager_id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id JOIN loan_payments lp ON l.id = lp.loan_id WHERE lp.status = 'late' GROUP BY e.full_name;

query: List all accounts opened in 2024 with a starting balance above EGP 50,000. Show account type, customer, branch, and current balance.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT at.name AS acct_type, c.full_name, b.name AS branch, a.account_number, a.balance FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id=c.id JOIN account_types at ON a.account_type_id=at.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id=b.id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y',a.opened_date)='2024' AND a.balance>50000 ORDER BY a.balance DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT a.account_number, c.full_name, b.name AS branch_name, at.name AS account_type, a.balance, a.currency FROM account_types at JOIN accounts a ON at.id = a.account_type_id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id WHERE a.opened_date >= '2024-01-01' AND a.balance > 50000 ORDER BY a.balance DESC;
full_sql_executed: SELECT at.name AS account_type, c.full_name AS customer_name, b.name AS branch_name, a.balance FROM account_types at JOIN accounts a ON at.id = a.account_type_id JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id WHERE a.opened_date >= '2024-01-01' AND a.balance > 50000;

query: For each branch, calculate the total interest income accrued from active loans (outstanding_balance × interest_rate / 12 for one month).
ground_truth_sql: SELECT b.name, ROUND(SUM(l.outstanding_balance*l.interest_rate/12),2) AS monthly_interest_income, COUNT(l.id) AS active_loans FROM loans l JOIN branches b ON l.branch_id=b.id WHERE l.status='active' GROUP BY b.id,b.name ORDER BY monthly_interest_income DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT b.name AS branch_name, SUM(l.outstanding_balance * l.interest_rate) AS total_interest_income FROM branches b JOIN customers c ON b.id = c.branch_id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id WHERE l.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.id, b.name ORDER BY total_interest_income DESC;
full_sql_executed: SELECT b.name AS branch_name, SUM(l.outstanding_balance * l.interest_rate) AS total_interest_income FROM branches b JOIN customers c ON b.id = c.branch_id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id WHERE l.status = 'active' GROUP BY b.id, b.name ORDER BY total_interest_income DESC;

pruned_sql_executed: SELECT DISTINCT c.full_name, a.account_number, a.account_type_id, a.last_activity, a.balance FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id WHERE a.status = 'frozen';

query: Show top 5 assets by total portfolio holdings count across all active portfolios, with their current combined market value.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT a.symbol, a.name, a.asset_class, COUNT(ph.id) AS num_holdings, ROUND(SUM(ph.current_value),2) AS total_market_value FROM portfolio_holdings ph JOIN assets a ON ph.asset_id=a.id JOIN portfolios p ON ph.portfolio_id=p.id WHERE p.is_active=1 GROUP BY a.id,a.symbol,a.name,a.asset_class ORDER BY num_holdings DESC LIMIT 5;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT a.symbol, a.name, a.asset_class, COUNT(ph.portfolio_id) AS portfolio_count FROM assets a JOIN portfolio_holdings ph ON a.id = ph.asset_id JOIN portfolios p ON ph.portfolio_id = p.id WHERE p.is_active = TRUE GROUP BY a.id, a.symbol, a.name, a.asset_class ORDER BY portfolio_count DESC LIMIT 5;
full_sql_executed: SELECT a.symbol, a.name, SUM(ph.quantity * mp.price) AS current_market_value FROM assets a JOIN market_prices mp ON a.id = mp.asset_id JOIN portfolio_holdings ph ON a.id = ph.asset_id GROUP BY a.id, a.symbol, a.name ORDER BY current_market_value DESC LIMIT 5;

query: Compute month-over-month transaction volume growth rate for completed transactions in 2024.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT month, total_vol, LAG(total_vol) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_vol, ROUND(100.0*(total_vol-LAG(total_vol) OVER (ORDER BY month))/NULLIF(LAG(total_vol) OVER (ORDER BY month),0),2) AS mom_growth_pct FROM (SELECT STRFTIME('%Y-%m',transaction_date) AS month, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) AS total_vol FROM transactions WHERE status='completed' AND STRFTIME('%Y',transaction_date)='2024' GROUP BY month) ORDER BY month;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT EXTRACT(MONTH FROM t.transaction_date) AS month, SUM(t.amount) AS total_volume
FROM transactions t
WHERE t.status = 'completed' AND t.transaction_date >= '2024-01-01' AND t.transaction_date < '2025-01-01'
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', t.transaction_date) AS month, COUNT(t.id) AS transaction_volume
FROM transactions t
WHERE t.status = 'completed' AND t.transaction_date >= '2024-01-01' AND t.transaction_date < '2025-01-01'
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

query: List all customers who joined in 2024 who already have at least one loan and at least one portfolio. Show their branch and product counts.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, b.name AS branch, COUNT(DISTINCT l.id) AS loan_count, COUNT(DISTINCT p.id) AS portfolio_count FROM customers c JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id=b.id JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id JOIN portfolios p ON p.customer_id=c.id WHERE STRFTIME('%Y',c.joined_date)='2024' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name,b.name HAVING loan_count>=1 AND portfolio_count>=1;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT DISTINCT c.id, c.full_name, b.name AS branch_name, COUNT(DISTINCT l.id) AS loan_count, COUNT(DISTINCT p.id) AS portfolio_count
FROM customers c
JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id
JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id
JOIN portfolios p ON c.id = p.customer_id
WHERE c.joined_date >= '2024-01-01' AND c.joined_date < '2025-01-01'
GROUP BY c.id, c.full_name, b.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.id) > 0 AND COUNT(DISTINCT p.id) > 0;
full_sql_executed:
SELECT DISTINCT c.id, c.full_name, b.name AS branch_name, COUNT(DISTINCT l.id) AS loan_count, COUNT(DISTINCT p.id) AS portfolio_count
FROM customers c
JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id
JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id
JOIN portfolios p ON c.id = p.customer_id
WHERE c.joined_date >= '2024-01-01' AND c.joined_date < '2025-01-01'
GROUP BY c.id, c.full_name, b.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.id) > 0 AND COUNT(DISTINCT p.id) > 0;

query: For each city, show the total outstanding loan balance, total account balance, and net lending position (loans minus deposits).
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.city, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT l.outstanding_balance),2) AS total_loans, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT a.balance),2) AS total_deposits, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT l.outstanding_balance)-SUM(DISTINCT a.balance),2) AS net_lending FROM customers c LEFT JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id AND l.status='active' LEFT JOIN accounts a ON a.customer_id=c.id AND a.status='active' GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY net_lending DESC;
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.city, SUM(l.outstanding_balance) AS total_outstanding FROM branches b JOIN customers c ON b.id = c.branch_id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY total_outstanding DESC;
full_sql_executed: SELECT c.city, SUM(l.outstanding_balance) AS total_outstanding FROM branches b JOIN customers c ON b.id = c.branch_id JOIN loans l ON c.id = l.customer_id GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY total_outstanding DESC;

query: For the Maadi Branch, produce a full customer 360 view: customer name, KYC status, number of accounts, total balance, active loans, and portfolio value.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.full_name, k.kyc_status, COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS acct_count, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT a.balance),2) AS total_balance, COUNT(DISTINCT l.id) AS loan_count, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT p.total_value),2) AS portfolio_value FROM customers c JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id=b.id LEFT JOIN kyc_records k ON k.customer_id=c.id LEFT JOIN accounts a ON a.customer_id=c.id AND a.status='active' LEFT JOIN loans l ON l.customer_id=c.id AND l.status='active' LEFT JOIN portfolios p ON p.customer_id=c.id AND p.is_active=1 WHERE b.name='Maadi Branch' GROUP BY c.id,c.full_name,k.kyc_status ORDER BY total_balance DESC;
expected_tables: customers, branches, kyc_records, accounts, loans, portfolios
pruned_sql_executed: SELECT c.full_name, l.outstanding_balance, p.total_value FROM accounts a JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id JOIN loans l ON a.id = l.disbursement_account_id JOIN portfolios p ON a.id = p.funding_account_id WHERE b.name = 'Maadi' AND l.status = 'active';
full_sql_executed:
SELECT c.full_name, l.outstanding_balance, p.total_value 
FROM accounts a 
JOIN customers c ON a.customer_id = c.id 
JOIN branches b ON c.branch_id = b.id 
JOIN loans l ON a.id = l.disbursement_account_id 
JOIN portfolios p ON a.id = p.funding_account_id 
WHERE b.name = 'Maadi' AND l.status = 'active';

query: Show all assets that appear in portfolios with a Conservative risk profile but are classified as Equity — a potential compliance breach. Include portfolio name, customer, and holding value.
ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.name AS portfolio, c.full_name AS customer, a.symbol, a.name AS asset_name, a.asset_class, ROUND(ph.current_value,2) AS holding_value FROM portfolios p JOIN portfolio_holdings ph ON ph.portfolio_id=p.id JOIN assets a ON ph.asset_id=a.id JOIN customers c ON p.customer_id=c.id WHERE p.risk_profile='Conservative' AND a.asset_class='Equity' AND p.is_active=1 ORDER BY ph.current_value DESC;
pruned_sql_executed:
SELECT assets.symbol, assets.name, portfolio_holdings.current_value, portfolios.name AS portfolio_name, customers.full_name AS customer_name 
FROM assets 
JOIN portfolio_holdings ON assets.id = portfolio_holdings.asset_id 
JOIN portfolios ON portfolio_holdings.portfolio_id = portfolios.id 
JOIN customers ON portfolios.customer_id = customers.id 
WHERE portfolios.risk_profile = 'Conservative' 
AND assets.asset_class = 'Equity';
full_sql_executed:
SELECT assets.symbol, assets.name, portfolios.name AS portfolio_name, customers.full_name AS customer_name, portfolio_holdings.current_value 
FROM assets 
JOIN portfolio_holdings ON assets.id = portfolio_holdings.asset_id 
JOIN portfolios ON portfolio_holdings.portfolio_id = portfolios.id 
JOIN customers ON portfolios.customer_id = customers.id 
WHERE assets.asset_class = 'Equity' 
AND portfolios.risk_profile = 'Conservative';
